title: Primitive Types 4
description: You can take a slice of an array using range syntax. Slices let you reference a contiguous sequence of elements.
instructions: Use a slice to get a portion of the array.
hints: The range is half-open: start is inclusive, end is exclusive.
explanation: A slice is a reference to a contiguous sequence of elements in a collection. The syntax `&a[1..4]` creates a slice starting at index 1 and ending before index 4 (exclusive). Slices don't own the data; they borrow it. The type of a slice of i32 values is `&[i32]`.
